sections.paragraph:
pret. מֵת (compare Syr. ܡܝܺܬ), מַתִּי.-

(1) to die (so in all the Phœnicio-Shemitic languages. The middle radical וּ appears to be softened from the liquid r, compare דָּרַשׁ, דּוּשׁ, etc., so that the original stock would be mrt, compare Sansc. mri, to die, mrita, dead, death; also mâth, muth, mith, mêth, mid, mêd, to kill; Malay, mita, to kill and to die; Zend. mreté, mereté; Pehlev. murdé;h, mard, mortal, man; Pers. مردن to die; Gr. μορτός, i.q. βροτός; Lat. mors, mortis, Morta, ap. Liv. Andr.; Germ. Mord; Old Germ. used not only for killing, but also for death). It is used of death, whether of men or beasts ( Exo 11:5 Ecc 9:4), whether natural ( Gen 5:8, 11 , 14 , 17 , 20 , 27 , 31 , and so very frequently) or violent, Exo 21:12, 15 Exo 21:15 Deu 13:10 19:11 , 12 Deu 19:12 21:21 Job 1:19, etc. The cause of death is put with the prefix בְּ, Jos 10:11, “more died בְּאַבְנֵי הַכָּרָד by the hail-stones than,” etc.; Jud. 15:18 , אָמוּת בַּצָּמָא “I die of thirst,” Eze 5:12 and with מִפְּנֵי Jer 38:9. Figuratively, the heart of any one is said to die, i.e. to fail, 1Sa 25:37 the trunk of a tree, Job 14:8 (compare הָרַג); land which lies untilled, Gen 47:19, “why should we die, we and our land,” which latter is then explained הָאֲדָמָה תֵשַׁם (compare Arab. مات to be untilled, sterile, desert, as land, Koran, ii. 159; xxv. 51; xxix. 63). Used ironically, Job 12:2, עִמָּכֶם תָּמוּת חָכְמָה “wisdom dies (i.e. is extinguished, perishes) with you.”

(2) to perish, to be destroyed, of a state, Amo 2:2 Hos 13:1, see מָוֶת .-Part. מֵת dying, about to die, Gen 20:3 dead, Num 19:11 13:16 without distinction of sex (as in Germ. ein Todter, ein Kranker, compare Hebr. Gram. § 105, 1 note), Gen 23:4. Pl. מֵתִים of idols, opp. of the living God (אֵל חַי), Psa 106:28.

Pilel מֹותֵת to slay, to kill, Psa 34:22 Jer 20:17 often emphat. to destroy thoroughly, kill out-right, 1Sa 14:13 Jud. 9:54 2Sa 1:16.

Hiphil הֵמִית, 2 pers. הֵמַתָּ, 1 pers. with suff. הֲמִיתִּיו 1Sa 17:35, הֲמִתִּיהָ Hos 2:3, id.; Jud. 16:30 2Sa 3:30 21:1 often used of death sent by God himself, by diseases, famine, etc. ( Isa 65:15 Hos 2:5 Exo 16:3 17:3 Num 14:15 16:13 ), sometimes opp. to the verb הָרַג, which is always used of violent death by the hand of man, comp. Isa 14:30 whence part. מְמִתִים killers, prob. angels of death, Job 33:22.

Hophal הוּמַת to be slain, Deu 21:22 1Sa 19:11.

Derivatives, מָמֹת, תְּמוּתָה, and-
